channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Transformative Forces: 5.5G Networks and AI Convergence in the Mobile AI Era
item.description: Convergence of 5.5G networks and AI marks a pivotal moment in technological evolution, reshaping our digital landscape.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Revolutionizing Agriculture: Unmanned Helicopters Poised to Change Industry
item.description: Unmanned helicopters, developed by companies like Rotor Technologies and Pyka, are changing the agriculture industry with their precision spraying capabilities and safety features.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Better Apple AirTags are coming – with new AirTag 2 rumors predicting these two big upgrades
item.description: An AirTag 2 has long been rumored, and a new report is shedding light on what to expect, and suggesting that it'll arrive in 2025.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: From Quibi’s ashes, new short-form streamers are thriving
item.description: Four years after Quibi’s $1.75 billion crashout, its successors are flying high. Apps like DramaBox and ReelShort are betting on short-form. They present feature-length films, diced into consumable minutes-long bites akin to a TikTok. The more clips you want to see, the more you’ll have to pay.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: About 20% of Americans regularly get their news from influencers on social media, report says
item.description: A report released Monday by Pew Research Center says about one in five Americans regularly get their news from so-called news influencers on social media.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The ‘Great IT Rebrand’: Restructuring IT for business success
item.description: Traditionally viewed as rock-solid and steady, the insurance industry is not exactly associated with taking big risks.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: DOGE: A Made-Up Department - Risks and Implications
item.description: The creation of DOGE (Department Of Getting Even) raises questions about its true purpose and potential consequences for the country.
item.dc:creator: Jay Willis, Fast Company

item.title: In Cyprus, Ukrainians learn how to dispose of landmines that kill and maim hundreds
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — In a Cypriot National Guard camp, Ukrainians are being trained on how to identify, locate and dispose of landmines and other unexploded munitions that litter huge swaths of their country, killing and maiming hundreds of people, including children.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: One Tech Tip: Replacing passwords with passkeys for an easier login experience
item.description: LONDON (AP) — If you're tired of memorizing passwords, then give passkeys a try. You might have noticed that many online services are now offering the option of using passkeys, a digital authentication method touted as an easier and more secure way to log in.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Inside the awe-inspiring 'Aurora' supercomputer at Argonne National Lab
item.description: Argonne National Laboratory's Aurora supercomputer is the fastest in the world, capable of a quintillion calculations per second, and is used to model everything from the structure of the universe to the structure of proteins essential to life.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lowe, WGN 9

item.title: Apple announces new feature to help airlines track your lost luggage
item.description: For air travelers who have popped an AirTag in their luggage – but still struggle to get help when suitcases are misplaced – this software update is for you.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: The Agile Manifesto was ahead of its time
item.description: Last week I went on a short rant about scrum, and how it isn’t agile and is, well, dumb. It occurred to me that one of the obstacles to truly being agile has been the limits of software technology. The way we had to build and deliver software didn’t lend itself to flexibility.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Chinese hackers target Tibetan websites in malware attack, cybersecurity group says
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A hacking group that is believed to be Chinese state-sponsored has compromised two websites with ties to the Tibetan community in an attack meant to install malware on users' computers, according to findings released Wednesday by a private cybersecurity firm.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Bitcoin neared $90,000 in a new record high. What to know about crypto's post-election rally
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As money continues to pour into crypto following Donald Trump's reelection last week, bitcoin has climbed to yet another record high. The world's largest cryptocurrency topped $89,000 for the first time, briefly peaking at $89,995 early Tuesday, according to CoinDesk.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Microsoft’s .NET 9 arrives, with performance, cloud, and AI boosts
item.description: Microsoft has announced the general availability of .NET 9, a major upgrade of its open source software development platform that features scads of performance enhancements and new capabilities for building cloud-native and generative AI-enabled applications. Released November 12, .
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: F5 gateway works to protect and manage AI applications
item.description: A new gateway from F5 is aimed at helping enterprises to better manage AI security and delivery. The F5 AI Gateway offers an automated way for customers to secure and manage interactions among AI applications, APIs, and large language models (LLMs).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Japan's SoftBank returns to profit after gains at Vision Fund and other investments
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese technology group SoftBank swung back to profitability in the July-September quarter, boosted by positive results in its Vision Fund investments. Tokyo-based SoftBank Group Corp.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, ABC 4
